parts.answer: The only correct answer is A (II → III, VII → II) B is incorrect because the final oxidation number for manganese is incorrect C is incorrect because the iron is oxidised and the manganese is reduced D is incorrect because the iron is oxidised and the initial and final oxidation numbers of manganese are incorrect (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C (0.450 mol dm – ) A is incorrect because this is the number of moles of FeSO in 25 cm B is incorrect because this is the concentration of manganate(VII) ions D is incorrect because this is the ratio of the two volumes (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B (2.72 V) A is incorrect because this is 0.5% lower than the cell potential C is incorrect because this is 1% higher than the cell potential D is incorrect because this 5% higher than the cell potential (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (Zn(s) ǀ Zn 2+ (aq) ǁ Ni 2+ (aq) ǀ Ni(s)) B is incorrect because the solid electrodes must be on the ends of the diagram C is incorrect because oxidation should be on the left D is incorrect because the solid electrodes must be on the ends of the diagram and oxidation should be on the left (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A () B is incorrect as this shows a negatively proportional relationship C is incorrect as this shows a curve D is incorrect as this line is not directly proportional (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B (mass spectrometry) A is incorrect as infrared data provide evidence for benzene’s structure C is incorrect as thermochemical data provide evidence for benzene’s structure and stability D is incorrect as X-ray diffraction data provide evidence for benzene’s structure (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B (HNO + 2H SO → NO + + 2HSO – + H O + ) A is incorrect because the reaction system is too acidic for the sulfate ion to be formed C is incorrect because no NO + ion is formed D is incorrect because hydroxide ion cannot exist in a strongly acidic solution (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is B (57.7%) A is incorrect because this is the percentage atom economy of HBr C is incorrect because this is the percentage of the mass of 2,4,6-tribromophenol compared to 3 bromine molecules D is incorrect because the HBr is included as a useful product (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is B (13.5 g) A is incorrect because this is 76.8% of 5.00 g C is incorrect because this is the value for a 100% yield D is incorrect because the value has been divided by 76.8 instead of multiplying (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C ( ) A is incorrect because this is the zwitterion B is incorrect because only one acid group has dissociated D is incorrect because the amino acid is fully protonated (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is D ( ) A is incorrect because the peptide link is not on either α-carbon atom B is incorrect because there is no aspartic acid monomer C is incorrect because the peptide link is not on the glutamine α-carbon (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (2) A is incorrect because there are two chiral centres on each B is incorrect because there are two chiral centres on each D is incorrect because there are two chiral centres on each (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is D (mineral oil) A is incorrect because the boiling temperature of water is below the melting temperature of aspirin B is incorrect because the boiling temperature of ethanol is below the melting temperature of aspirin C is incorrect because the boiling temperature of hexane is below the melting temperature of aspirin (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is D (2-hydroxybenzoic acid) A is incorrect because ethanoic acid is soluble in water and won’t crystallise B is incorrect because ethanoic anhydride is hydrolysed by water and won’t crystallise C is incorrect because ethanol is soluble in water and won’t crystallise (1) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C (magnesium) A is incorrect because the metal used is magnesium B is incorrect because the metal used is magnesium D is incorrect because the metal used is magnesium (1) number Mark

The only correct answer is C (nucleophile) A is incorrect because it is not a redox reaction B is incorrect because it is not a redox reaction D is incorrect because the reagent acts as a nucleophile (1) number Mark

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points: • number of steps in each reaction sequence • purification required (after each step) • availability/cost of reagents/catalysts • toxicity of reagents/intermediates/by-products • atom economy (of the product) • (%) yield of each step • side reactions • carbon dioxide emissions • consideration of reaction conditions Allow separation of mixtures Allow hazardous reagents/intermediates/by-products Accept yield of (final) product Allow formation of byproducts Allow emissions of greenhouse gases Allow (total) energy required Allow cost of (reaction) conditions Allow high pressures/temperatures Ignore unjustified costs/pollution/safety/yield/efficiency Ignore rates of reaction/time number Answer Additional guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points: • (the molecule contains two atoms of chlorine so) a 9:6:1 ratio is seen (1) • because peak at 162 contains two Cl isotopes, peak at 164 contains one Cl and one Cl, and peak at 166 contains two Cl isotopes (1) Allow Cl: Cl ratio is 3:1 Allow Cl is more abundant than Cl Allow three isotope combinations without m/z values number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • (the presence of 1 atom of a) carbon-13 isotope Allow the presence of (1 atom of) a hydrogen-2 isotope / deuterium Ignore M+1 / n+1 peaks (Total for Question 15 = 12 marks) number Answer Additional guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following points: • correct species (1) • balanced and state symbols (1) An example of an equation [Fe(H O) ] 3+ (aq) +3OH – (aq) → [Fe(OH) (H O) ](s) + 3H O(l) Allow Fe(OH) (s) + 6H O(l) Allow sodium carbonate 2[Fe(H O) ] 3+ (aq) + 3CO – (aq) → Fe (CO ) (s) + 12H O(l) Allow ammonia [Fe(H O) ] 3+ (aq) + 3NH (aq) → [Fe(OH) (H O) ](s) + 3NH + (aq) Allow uncancelled cations Allow NaOH on LHS with Na + on RHS Square brackets are not required on the RHS number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: • 2+ / +2 (1) • bidentate (1) • 6 and octahedral (1) Ignore 2 Do not award didentate Ignore multidentate number Answer Additional guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to: • equation (1) • ΔS system increases as there are more particles (4 to 7) (1) [Ni(H O) ] 2+ + 3(CH ) NCS − → [Ni((CH ) NCS ) ] – + 6H O Allow Na + as a spectator ion in a balanced equation Ignore state symbols even if is incorrect Ignore extra charges on ligands in the complex Allow S/entropy increases as there are more moles Ignore “more products than reactants” Do not award more molecules TE on incorrect numbers in equation with entropy increasing Marks are independent number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes: • calculation of relative formula mass of precipitate (1) • calculation of mass in grams in 1 dm (1) • calculation of concentration (1) Example of a calculation: 58.7 + 3(120.2) + 1 = 420.3 245 ÷ 1000 ÷ 5 = 0.049 / 4.9 × 10 –2 (g dm – ) 0.049 ÷ 420.3 = 0.000117 / 1.17  10 –4 (mol dm – ) TE throughout Alternative M2 and M3: 245 ÷ 1000 ÷ 420.3 = 0.000583 / 5.83  10 –4 (mol) 5.83  10 –4 ÷ 5 = 0.000117 / 1.17  10 –4 (mol dm –3 ) Ignore SF except 1SF Correct answer with some working scores 3 (Total for Question 16 = 10 marks) number Answer Additional guidance Mark

An explanation that makes reference to the following points: • this reaction produces (a pair of) enantiomers / a racemic mixture / racemate (1) • the molecule is planar around the C=N bond (1) • the CN – /nucleophile can attack from either side / both sides / above or below (the double bond) (1) Allow optical(ly active) isomers Allow “molecules that are (non-superimposable) mirror images” Do not award optically active solution Ignore “different” isomers Ignore chiral carbon Allow planar around reaction site Allow (trigonal) planar around the δ+ C Do not award “planar carbocation” Ignore left and right Marks are independent number Answer Additional guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following points: • hydrolysis (1) • aqueous acid solution / H + (aq) (1) • reflux (1) Ignore modifiers Allow specific aqueous named strong acids or formulae Allow aqueous alkali solution followed by neutralisation with acid Do not award concentrated acid Further reagents negate M2 Allow heat M3 dependent on M1 or M2 (Total for Question 17 = 9 marks) number Answer Additional guidance Mark *18 This question assesses the student’s ability to show a coherent and logically structured answer with linkages and fully sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicative content and for how the answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for indicative content. Number of indicative marking points seen in answer Number of marks awarded for indicative marking points 6 4 5-4 3 3-2 2 1 1 0 0 The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for structure and lines of reasoning Number of marks awarded for structure of answer and sustained lines of reasoning Answer shows a coherent logical structure with linkages and fully sustained lines of reasoning demonstrated throughout Answer is partially structured with some linkages and lines of reasoning Answer has no linkages between points and is unstructured Guidance on how the mark scheme should be applied. The mark for indicative content should be added to the mark for lines of reasoning. For example, a response with five indicative marking points that is partially structured with some linkages and lines of reasoning scores 4 marks (3 marks for indicative content and 1 mark for partial structure and some linkages and lines of reasoning). If there were no linkages between the points, then the same indicative marking points would yield an overall score of 3 marks (3 marks for indicative content and no marks for linkages). In general it would be expected that 5 or 6 indicative points would get 2 reasoning marks, and 3 or 4 ind

An answer that makes reference to the following points: • calculation of atoms of carbon per molecule (1) • calculation of atoms of hydrogen (1) • identification of C=O bond peak with range/value or identification of a functional group with its range (1) • identification of C=C bond peak with its range/value or alkene with its range (1) • correct displayed structure (1) Example of calculation: 95.6 ÷ 24 = 3.98 so 4 moles CO (per mole of molecules) / 4 carbon atoms per molecule 54.1 ÷ 18 = 3.005 moles of water so (×2) = 6 hydrogen atoms per molecule (Peak P (1735 cm – )) Allow any number(s) in the range 1760 – 1700 cm – , functional groups can be ignored (aldehyde) 1740 – 1720 cm –1 , (ester) 1750 – 1735 cm –1 , (ketones) 1720 – 1700 cm –1 Accept absorbances labelled on spectrum Peak Q (1645 cm – ) is in the range 1669 – 1645 Accept absorbances labelled on spectrum Allow numbers outside the range if identified as alkyl C=C If no correct ranges or wavelengths are given then 1 mark can be scored for the identification of both bonds. C C O C C O H H H H H H Allow any compound with 4 carbons and 6 hydrogens and at least one C=O. This does not have to match previous conclusions except: If C=O is not identified then butadiene could score M5 as a TE Do not award acid structures Do not award structures that omit a C=C bond Ignore names even if incorrect number Answer Additional guidance Mark
